rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 свщмч., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар свщмч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря свщмч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. свщмч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар свщмч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря свщмч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і свщмч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний свщмч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар свщмч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря свщмч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. свщмч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – свщмч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Кор. 138 зач.; 7, 24-35. Єв. – Мт. 61 зач.; 15, 12-21.

troparion_en.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Pancratius entreat Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion_en.text: Pancratius, you were revealed as a brilliant star for the people of Taormina; / you were also shown to be a sufferer for Christ. / Since you now stand before Him, O blessed one, pray for those who honor you.

matched_oca_saint: Hieromartyr Pancratius, Bishop of Taormina in Sicily

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Pancratius, Bishop of Taormina in Sicily

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Pancratius entreat Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion.text: Pancratius, you were revealed as a brilliant star for the people of Taormina; / you were also shown to be a sufferer for Christ. / Since you now stand before Him, O blessed one, pray for those who honor you.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Cyril, Bishop of Gortyna in Crete

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Cyril, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Patermuthius, Coprius, and Alexander the Soldier, in Egypt

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Patermuthius, Coprius, and Alexander, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Against your will, you received your flock as a Hierarch,  / but of your own free will you contested for the Church,  / and the unbelieving Caliph Mavi converted to the Faith with the help of earth from the Lord's tomb.  / Along with his physical blindness, you also healed his soul, and instructed him in piety. /  At the same time you drove heretics away from the Church like wolves. /  O most revered Hierarch Theodore,  / pray that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: By your good will, you graciously illumined your child,  / the revered Mavi, at the Life-Giving Tree.  / You went to Constantinople, /  where you healed the eyes of the virtuous Empress Theodora with earth from the Lord's tomb and restored her sight.  / From there, with the desired treasure again in Babylon  / you strengthened the honorable Mavi for his martyrdom for Christ;  / therefore we cry out to you: /  “Rejoice, revered Theodore,  / disciple of Christ, the Good Shepherd.”

oca_saints.name: Saint Dionysios the Rhetorician and his disciple Saint Mētrophánēs

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “The Divine Faith...”)
You consumed the roots of the passions with divine coals,  / and shone ascetically on Athos,  / for radiant with the unwaning light,  / you were shown as equals to the God-bearers.  / O wise Dionysios and godly Mētrophánēs, entreat Christ God that great mercy may be granted unto us.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
As most sacred initiates of the Trinity, and of equal standing, and communicants with the Monastic Saints,  / O wise Dionysios and Mētrophánēs,  / never cease to quash the schemes of the Enemy against us,  / so that we may cry: “Rejoice, O God-bearers.”
